rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Hear The Petition For Order Of Affirmation In Application For Italian Dual Citizenship; Declaration Of Phillip Mark Edginton In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2024, Line 1, PETITIONER PHILLIP EDGINTON's Motion to Hear the Petition For Order of Affirmation In Application For Italian Dual Citizenship. Petitioner's unopposed "petition for order in affirmation in application for dual Italian citizenship" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY LEWIS VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2024, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JERRY LEWIS's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. The report and recommendation is adopted as follows: This is a lemon law case. Defendant withheld numerous documents alleging that the requests were impermissible. The prevailing authority regarding the scope of permissible discovery allows for the type of discovery sought by Plaintiff. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Company (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138; Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 967; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334.) For example, Plaintiff requests electronically stored information ("ESI") from Nissan North America Inc.'s ("NNA") multiple electronic databases, inter alia, documents from NNA's warranty databases, customer complaint databases, technician line database, emails between NNA's engineers and other entities such as NHTSA, for the same or similar defects in other similarly situated vehicles to establish NNA was aware of the defects and was unable to fix the defects despite knowing it was unable to do so. (See Jensen v. BMW of N. Am., LLC, (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 112, 136.) Defendant's objections are not supported by relevant case law. The motion is granted. Defendant shall produce all non-privileged requested documents within 10 days of the entry of this order. If there are privileged documents, a privilege log shall be produced. To the extent Defendant contends that the production of documents would be burdensome, that objection is denied. There was no declaration from Defendant, as opposed to its attorney, which details the alleged burden involved. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BOULWARE VS. ABC TILE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2024, Line 11, DEFENDANT SPECTRUM QUARTZ LLC's Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction. Spectrum Quartz, LLC's unopposed "motion to quash service for lack of jurisdiction" is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. ARNALDO ANDRE LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
